steps.title: Apply the triangle angle sum property
steps.content:
In any triangle, the three interior angles sum to 180°. For triangle JKL:
$$\angle J + \angle K + \angle L = 180°$$

steps.title: Substitute and solve for angle J
steps.content:
Given $\angle K = 43°$ and $\angle L = 43°$, substitute:
$$\angle J + 43° + 43° = 180°$$
Simplify:
$$\angle J = 180° - 86° = 94°$$
The measure of $\angle J$ is 94 degrees.
